q: Water transport in plants: Which combination of forces drives the movement of water from roots to leaves via xylem?
options: Root pressure alone forces water up via osmosis at each xylem vessel junction
Capillary action is the sole mechanism; adhesion to xylem walls pulls water all the way to the canopy
Transpiration pull (tension from evaporation) combined with cohesion among water molecules and adhesion to xylem walls, supplemented by root pressure
Active transport pumps embedded in xylem vessels use ATP to push water toward leaves
explanation: The cohesion-tension model combines: transpiration pull creating negative pressure, cohesion keeping the water column intact, adhesion preventing column breakage, and root pressure contributing at the base.
